lines.text: (approaching) Earl. [pause] It's froze solid out there.
[flatly] Gunny.
Coffee's already going.
You slept out here again.
[flatly] Percolator's ahead of you. Get yourself a cup.
I asked did you sleep out here.
[quietly] There's a chair by the trainer.
(approaching) That road past the culvert is a sheet of ice, I come up on it near sideways. [chuckles] Evening, Earl. Gunny.
My defroster quit back in November. I drove the whole way with the window cracked.
Dutch. Stitch. Coffee's on.
[deliberate] All right. Barn's secured, coffee's poured. We'll open her up.
[deliberate] Apollo Eighteen. Lunar descent rehearsal. Number two thousand, eight hundred and six.
Here.
Present.
Accounted for.
I stayed up Sunday night and watched that landing again. [pause] The whole business. That boy they run the parade for down in Houston.
Computer flew it, Gunny. Flew the approach, found its own spot, and set it down, and he just had his hands in his lap the whole way.
[deliberate] If you're bringing a case you'll bring it in order. Cricket's the bench, same as he always is.
That's what I'm doing. I'm prosecuting. Cricket, I want a finding out of you tonight.
[flatly] Go ahead.
(reading) [deliberate] He had it in hand from four hundred feet. October the ninth. Says so right there where I wrote it down.
[sarcastic] Four hundred feet. All right, four hundred feet of flying on a landing the box already had finished for him.
[deliberate] Three hundred and eighty. I took it off the loop myself, so we'll use the real one.
See now, that there is about the last little sliver of it, and they hang a medal on the man and march him down the street.
[deliberate] One man at a time. We hear the figure, then we hear the argument. There's an order to a proceeding, McGrew.
I've heard the figure. In my court a computer landing doesn't earn a fella a parade. Call that a rule of evidence.
[flatly] There isn't any such rule.
[chuckles] There is now. Entered it just this second.
You can't go entering rules in the middle of your own prosecution.
[warmly] I'm the prosecutor and the historian both, Gunny, so I surely can.
Cricket. [pause] Does hand-flying from three hundred and eighty count for the man or don't it.
[flatly] Manual's manual. Count it.
The bench counts it, and I'll tell you it still doesn't come to a whole lot.
Here's my finding. [pause] Not a one of these kids they're decorating would've got past the flight surgeon in our day, and you all know that as well as I do.
[flatly] That part's true. On the medical alone they'd have washed every one of them out.
[deliberate] Is that the finding of this crew, then.
Concur.
Dawes.
[deliberate] Let the record show he concurs.
All right. Cups in the sink. Mind the cord on the trainer going out.
[slowly] Net closed. Same time Tuesday.
[quietly] God willing.
